{"society_power": 7.0, "state_power": 7.5, "society_change": -0.5, "state_change": -0.5, "key_event": "Cold War's end, deregulation, and declining associational life", "reasoning": "The end of the Cold War, defense drawdown, financial/telecom deregulation, and welfare reform modestly weakened the state's regulatory and national-security capacities even as the carceral state grew; society was pulled between declining unions/associational life (\"bowling alone\") and new but fragmented, polarized mobilization channels, netting a slight decline."}
